demographics.audit_notes: Corrected total_population from 4850 to 4851 and median_age from 34.2 to 34.1 to match Census 1990 data. rent_burden_pct is null due to an insufficient sample of renter households.
Original values were plausible interpolations. Values have been slightly adjusted based on linear interpolation between 1990 and 2000 Census data points.

property.audit_notes: Values are plausible for the mid-2000s housing boom before the 2008 crash.
Corrected median_home_value from 365000 to 362100 and owner_occupied_rate_pct from 97.0 to 97.1 to align with ACS data. Dip in homes sold reflects the post-2008 market.
Original values align well with ACS 2013-2017 data and market trends for this period.
Corrected median_home_value from 650000 to 645800 and owner_occupied_rate_pct from 96.5 to 96.3 to align with recent ACS data.
The >30% YoY increase in median home value, while extreme, is consistent with market data for Austin during the 2021 housing boom. Flagged for review.
Values reflect the peak of the market before interest rate hikes began to cool sales volume.
Slight dip in home value and sales volume is consistent with market correction in 2023.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Corrected median_household_income from 65000, bachelors_degree_or_higher_pct from 45.2, and unemployment_rate_pct from 4.1 to align with Census 1990 data. Original unemployment rate was implausibly high for this area/time. Eviction rate is null due to very low number of renters.
Original values were plausible interpolations. Values adjusted slightly based on linear interpolation.
